DoCoTe Web MVP

Turn code changes into clear documentation follow-up and release-ready outputs.

DoCoTe helps teams connect pull requests, branches, and commit ranges to the documentation targets, drafts, and release guidance they likely need next.

First user flow

  1. Sign in to DoCoTe
  2. Connect GitHub
  3. Select repository and PR / branch / commit range
  4. Optionally add Jira ticket text or current documentation text
  5. Generate technical summary, release summary, and documentation update draft
  6. Review likely impacted documentation areas

GitHub connection

This is the first OAuth/config skeleton for the web-first DoCoTe track. It does not yet complete a real login flow, but it defines the route structure and configuration checks needed for real repository access.

example-org/payments-platform

Available branch options

This flow now reads from mocked GitHub-style backend endpoints and derives branch/PR selection options. Next steps are OAuth, real repository access, and commit-range selection.

Repository insights

Repository-to-doc map

Commit range concept

Later, DoCoTe should support analysis not only from PRs and branches, but also from explicit commit ranges.

Current label: abc1234...def5678

Documentation source

The current prototype uses manual documentation text, but the product is designed to evolve toward richer doc sources.

Current source: manual-text

Current prototype mode

The current web-first DoCoTe prototype is still running in mocked analysis mode, but the backend structure is being shaped so it can later switch to a provider-backed and repo-backed flow with fewer architectural changes.

Interactive demo flow

Current analysis target:
Repository: example-org/payments-platform
Scope type: pull-request
Scope reference: #148

Analysis readiness

Ready to analyze

Suggested demo flow

  1. Show repo and scope context
  2. Run the analysis flow
  3. Read the MVP highlights
  4. Review impacted documentation targets
  5. Check release readiness and review lane
  6. Open structured deliverables
  7. Use export/share for handoff

Jira context state

Latest stored Jira / ticket context for this prototype session:

No Jira context stored yet.

Current documentation context

Latest stored current-document text for this prototype session:

No current documentation text stored yet.

Technical summary

This change updates beneficiary validation rules and modifies payment review-state handling. The implementation appears to tighten validation and align workflow transitions with the newer review process.

Release summary

Updated beneficiary validation and payment review flow. Teams should verify documentation and operational guidance related to payment review handling.

Documentation draft

## Beneficiary validation update The validation rules were updated to support the revised payment review process. ## Review workflow Payment review states now follow the newer transition logic and should be reflected in operational documentation.

Likely impacted documentation areas

  • Payment validation documentation
  • Operations / review workflow guidance
  • Release notes for payment flow changes

Recent analysis history

No analyses recorded yet in this prototype session.

Debug history

No debug snapshots recorded yet.

Code-aware summaries

Turn PRs and commit ranges into readable technical implementation summaries.

Release-ready output

Generate concise release summaries that can be reused by delivery and product teams.

Documentation impact view

Highlight which documentation areas are likely affected by a code change.

Preserved Jira track

Keep the earlier Jira-first app as a future integration surface, not wasted work.
